content:
## Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides implementation guidance for the Core Information Standard v2.0, a comprehensive healthcare data model developed using WRIST (Wonderful Ramsey Systems Information Specification Tool). The standard defines a structured framework for capturing essential person-centred health and care information across the UK healthcare system.

The Core Information Standard encompasses demographics, clinical history, assessments, care planning, and administrative data required for continuity of care. It supports interoperability between healthcare systems while maintaining focus on person-centred care principles.

## Document Overview

This DAK is structured to support progressive implementation of the Core Information Standard. Phase 1 establishes the foundational elements including key health interventions, generic personas for implementation teams, and essential references. Subsequent phases will provide detailed technical specifications, implementation patterns, and validation guidance.

The standard leverages established UK healthcare terminologies including SNOMED CT for clinical concepts, NHS Data Dictionary for administrative elements, dm+d for medications, and UCUM for units of measure, ensuring alignment with existing NHS digital infrastructure.
title: Introduction

content:
## Key Health Interventions

The Core Information Standard supports evidence-based healthcare delivery through structured data capture aligned with clinical best practices. The following interventions represent priority areas for standardised information management:

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person-centred care planning | Implement structured goal-setting and outcome tracking using standardised templates | NICE NG86 | A |
| Medication reconciliation | Capture complete medication history including over-the-counter medications using dm+d codes | NICE NG5 | A |
| Risk assessment documentation | Standardise risk documentation using SNOMED CT codes for consistency across care settings | CQC guidance | B |
| Individual requirements capture | Document reasonable adjustments and accessibility needs systematically | Equality Act 2010 | Legal requirement |
| Advance care planning | Record preferences for future care including CPR decisions and preferred place of care | GMC guidance | B |
| Family history recording | Capture relevant family medical history using structured clinical codes | NICE CG164 | C |
| Safeguarding documentation | Maintain comprehensive safeguarding records with appropriate access controls | Care Act 2014 | Legal requirement |
| Contact information management | Ensure accurate professional and personal contact details for care coordination | NHS Long Term Plan | B |

These interventions are supported by the standard's comprehensive data model, enabling consistent capture and sharing of critical health information across care boundaries.
title: Health Interventions & Recommendations

content:
## Implementation Team Personas

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | Clinical Information Manager | Healthcare professional responsible for clinical data governance and standards implementation | Yes | Clinical knowledge, data standards, information governance |
| CISV2.P2 | Health IT System Analyst | Technical analyst specialising in healthcare system integration and data mapping | Yes | FHIR, HL7, healthcare interoperability, system analysis |
| CISV2.P3 | Clinical Safety Officer | Medical professional ensuring patient safety aspects of digital implementations | Yes | Clinical risk management, patient safety, regulatory compliance |
| CISV2.P4 | Data Protection Officer | Privacy specialist managing GDPR compliance and data sharing governance | Yes | Data protection law, privacy impact assessment, consent management |
| CISV2.P5 | Implementation Project Manager | Project manager coordinating cross-functional teams for standard deployment | Yes | Healthcare project management, change management, stakeholder engagement |

### Clinical Information Manager (CISV2.P1)
The Clinical Information Manager serves as the bridge between clinical practice and technical implementation. They ensure that data structures accurately reflect clinical workflows and that coded terminology aligns with established practices. Their expertise in both healthcare delivery and information governance makes them essential for validating that the standard meets clinical needs while maintaining data quality.

### Health IT System Analyst (CISV2.P2)
Health IT System Analysts translate clinical requirements into technical specifications. They possess deep understanding of healthcare interoperability standards and are responsible for mapping existing system data to the Core Information Standard format. Their technical expertise ensures seamless integration with existing NHS digital infrastructure.

### Clinical Safety Officer (CISV2.P3)
Clinical Safety Officers evaluate patient safety implications of implementing the standard. They conduct clinical risk assessments, ensure that information flows support safe clinical decision-making, and validate that critical safety information is appropriately captured and communicated across care settings.

### Data Protection Officer (CISV2.P4)
Data Protection Officers ensure that implementation complies with GDPR and other privacy regulations. They oversee consent management processes, conduct privacy impact assessments, and establish governance frameworks for appropriate information sharing while protecting individual privacy rights.

### Implementation Project Manager (CISV2.P5)
Implementation Project Managers coordinate the complex, multi-stakeholder process of deploying the standard across healthcare organisations. They manage timelines, resources, and stakeholder communications while ensuring that implementation remains aligned with strategic objectives and clinical priorities.
title: Generic Personas

content:
## Standards and Specifications

1. NHS Data Dictionary. *Data Elements and Attributes*. Available at: [https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/](https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/)
2. SNOMED International. *SNOMED CT Browser*. Available at: [https://browser.ihtsdotools.org/](https://browser.ihtsdotools.org/)
3. NHS Digital. *dm+d (Dictionary of Medicines and Devices)*. Available at: [https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd](https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd)
4. Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM). Available at: [https://ucum.org/](https://ucum.org/)
5. HL7 FHIR R4. *Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources*. Available at: [https://www.hl7.org/fhir/](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/)

## Clinical Guidelines

6. NICE. (2022). *Shared decision making* (NG197). National Institute for Health and Care Excellence.
7. NICE. (2015). *Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines* (NG5). National Institute for Health and Care Excellence.
8. NICE. (2021). *Patient experience in adult NHS services* (CG138). National Institute for Health and Care Excellence.
9. Care Quality Commission. (2019). *Guidance for providers on meeting the fundamental standards*. CQC.

## Legislation and Policy

10. UK Government. (2010). *Equality Act 2010*. HMSO.
11. UK Government. (2014). *Care Act 2014*. HMSO.
12. European Parliament. (2016). *General Data Protection Regulation* (EU 2016/679).
13. NHS England. (2019). *The NHS Long Term Plan*. NHS England.
14. UK Government. (2005). *Mental Capacity Act 2005*. HMSO.

## Professional Guidance

15. General Medical Council. (2020). *Treatment and care towards the end of life: good practice in decision making*. GMC.
16. Professional Record Standards Body. (2018). *Clinical document indexing standards*. PRSB.
17. NHS Digital. (2021). *Information Governance Toolkit*. NHS Digital.
18. British Computer Society. (2020). *Healthcare IT Professional Standards*. BCS.
title: References

title: Core Data Elements

content:
## Clinical Information Management Processes

The Core Information Standard supports structured healthcare workflows that ensure person-centred care delivery while maintaining clinical safety and regulatory compliance. These processes define how clinical teams capture, validate, and utilise comprehensive health information across care settings.

### Process Design Principles

- **Person-centred approach**: All processes prioritise individual preferences and involve patients in decision-making
- **Clinical safety**: Critical information such as allergies and risks must be captured and communicated effectively
- **Information governance**: Data handling complies with GDPR and clinical information governance standards
- **Interoperability**: Structured data capture enables seamless information sharing between systems
- **Professional accountability**: Clear assignment of clinical responsibility for data accuracy and care decisions

These processes support clinical teams in delivering coordinated, evidence-based care while ensuring comprehensive documentation meets both clinical and regulatory requirements.

activities.objectives: Capture comprehensive patient demographics, clinical history, and establish care baseline while ensuring data quality and clinical safety

processes_data.summary_markdown: The following business processes demonstrate how healthcare professionals capture and utilise Core Information Standard data to deliver person-centred care. Each process shows the flow of clinical activities, data collection points, and professional responsibilities required for safe, effective healthcare delivery.

title: Business Processes & Workflows

content:
## New Patient Registration and Initial Assessment

A 45-year-old patient with multiple chronic conditions arrives at a new GP practice after relocating. The practice needs to establish comprehensive baseline information while ensuring continuity of care from their previous provider.

**Personas involved:** Clinical Information Manager (CISV2.P1), Health IT System Analyst (CISV2.P2), Data Protection Officer (CISV2.P4)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.A, CISV2.D

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE4 | Person first name | Patient identification and registration |
| CISV2.DE5 | Person family name | Patient identification and registration |
| CISV2.DE10 | Date of birth | Patient identification and age verification |
| CISV2.DE15 | NHS number | Unique patient identification |
| CISV2.DE19 | Address line 1 | Current residence verification |
| CISV2.DE24 | Postcode | Geographic location for service allocation |
| CISV2.DE29 | Person's telephone number | Primary contact method |
| CISV2.DE50 | GP practice details | Previous provider information |
| CISV2.DE215 | Consent for information sharing | Legal basis for record transfer |
| CISV2.DE640 | Problem list record entry | Existing health conditions |
| CISV2.DE1315 | Medication item cluster | Current medication regime |

## Complex Care Planning for Multiple Conditions

A patient with diabetes, heart failure, and mobility issues requires comprehensive care coordination involving multiple specialists. The care team must develop an integrated plan addressing medical management, social support, and quality of life goals.

**Personas involved:** Clinical Information Manager (CISV2.P1), Clinical Safety Officer (CISV2.P3), Implementation Project Manager (CISV2.P5)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.B, CISV2.A

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE654 | Problem | Documented health conditions |
| CISV2.DE1440 | Goals and hopes | Patient-centred care objectives |
| CISV2.DE1445 | What | Specific care actions required |
| CISV2.DE1448 | Who | Responsible care team members |
| CISV2.DE1452 | When | Care delivery timelines |
| CISV2.DE1189 | Structured assessment name | Functional capacity evaluation |
| CISV2.DE1191 | Assessment value | Quantified health status measures |
| CISV2.DE453 | Professional contacts record entry | Care team coordination |
| CISV2.DE1481 | Planned review date/interval | Care plan monitoring schedule |
| CISV2.DE1242 | Risk | Identified safety concerns |
| CISV2.DE155 | Mobility needs | Functional support requirements |

## Medication Safety and Reconciliation

A patient presents to the emergency department with suspected adverse drug reaction. The clinical team needs rapid access to complete medication history, known allergies, and prescribing information to ensure safe treatment decisions.

**Personas involved:** Clinical Information Manager (CISV2.P1), Clinical Safety Officer (CISV2.P3), Health IT System Analyst (CISV2.P2)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.C, CISV2.A

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1330 | Medication name | Current prescribed medications |
| CISV2.DE1341 | Date supplied | Medication dispensing timeline |
| CISV2.DE1367 | Course status | Active/discontinued medication status |
| CISV2.DE1268 | Causative agent | Known allergy triggers |
| CISV2.DE1279 | Description of reaction | Adverse reaction details |
| CISV2.DE1283 | Severity | Clinical impact assessment |
| CISV2.DE199 | Alert | Critical safety warnings |
| CISV2.DE1328 | Medication prescribed by | Prescribing authority verification |
| CISV2.DE1355 | Dose | Medication dosing information |
| CISV2.DE1343 | Route | Administration method |
| CISV2.DE1368 | Indication | Clinical rationale for prescription |

## Secure Information Sharing During Care Transition

A patient requires transfer from acute hospital care to community rehabilitation services. Multiple organisations need coordinated information sharing while maintaining appropriate consent and data protection standards.

**Personas involved:** Data Protection Officer (CISV2.P4), Health IT System Analyst (CISV2.P2), Implementation Project Manager (CISV2.P5)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.D, CISV2.B

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE215 | Consent for information sharing | Legal basis for data transfer |
| CISV2.DE558 | Date of discharge | Care transition timing |
| CISV2.DE571 | Discharge destination | Receiving care provider |
| CISV2.DE476 | Referral details record entry | Inter-service communication |
| CISV2.DE1406 | Plan and requested actions record entry | Ongoing care requirements |
| CISV2.DE453 | Professional contacts record entry | Receiving care team details |
| CISV2.DE1266 | Type of reaction | Critical safety information |
| CISV2.DE1315 | Medication item cluster | Treatment continuity |
| CISV2.DE1181 | Assessments record entry | Functional status documentation |
| CISV2.DE1799 | Confidentiality | Information sensitivity classification |
| CISV2.DE262 | Mental capacity assessment | Decision-making capacity status |

Complex Care Planning for Multiple Conditions
Secure Information Sharing During Care Transition

title: User Scenarios

content:
## Clinical Decision Support Tables

The Core Information Standard includes decision support logic to guide healthcare professionals in making informed clinical decisions. These tables reference specific data elements to trigger appropriate clinical actions and safety checks.

### DT-01: Medication Safety Decision Support

**Description**: Automated decision support for medication prescribing and administration safety

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Logic | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Known allergy present | CISV2.DE1268 (Causative agent), CISV2.DE1330 (Medication name) | IF medication contains known allergen | Display critical alert, prevent prescription |
| Duplicate medication | CISV2.DE1330 (Medication name), CISV2.DE1367 (Course status) | IF active medication with same therapeutic class | Warn of potential duplication, suggest review |
| High-risk medication | CISV2.DE1330 (Medication name), CISV2.DE10 (Date of birth) | IF high-risk drug AND patient age >65 | Require dose adjustment or alternative |
| Interaction risk | CISV2.DE1330 (Medication name), existing medications | IF drug-drug interaction detected | Display interaction warning, suggest monitoring |

### DT-02: Safeguarding Risk Assessment

**Description**: Automated risk identification for vulnerable patients requiring additional safeguarding measures

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Logic | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Child at risk | CISV2.DE10 (Date of birth), CISV2.DE387 (Safeguarding concerns) | IF age <18 AND safeguarding concerns present | Trigger child protection pathway |
| Mental capacity concern | CISV2.DE262 (Mental capacity assessment), CISV2.DE10 (Date of birth) | IF capacity assessment indicates impairment | Ensure appropriate consent processes |
| Vulnerable adult | CISV2.DE387 (Safeguarding concerns), CISV2.DE1242 (Risk) | IF safeguarding concerns AND risk factors present | Activate adult safeguarding procedures |

### DT-03: Care Transition Risk Management

**Description**: Decision support for safe care transitions between services and settings

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Logic | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| High readmission risk | CISV2.DE654 (Problem), CISV2.DE558 (Date of discharge) | IF multiple comorbidities AND recent discharge | Arrange enhanced community follow-up |
| Incomplete medication reconciliation | CISV2.DE1315 (Medication item cluster), CISV2.DE558 (Date of discharge) | IF discharge WITHOUT complete medication review | Mandate pharmacy reconciliation |
| Missing essential information | CISV2.DE215 (Consent for information sharing), CISV2.DE476 (Referral details) | IF referral WITHOUT adequate clinical information | Request additional documentation before transfer |

### DT-04: Emergency Clinical Alerts

**Description**: Critical alert generation for emergency situations requiring immediate clinical attention

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Logic | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Resuscitation status | CISV2.DE1622 (CPR decision), emergency admission | IF DNR order present AND emergency situation | Display prominent DNR status to clinical team |
| Mental Health Act status | CISV2.DE333 (Mental health act status), location change | IF sectioned under MHA AND location change proposed | Ensure legal compliance before transfer |
| Critical allergy | CISV2.DE1268 (Causative agent), CISV2.DE1283 (Severity) | IF life-threatening allergy documented | Display prominent allergy warning on all screens |
| High-risk patient | CISV2.DE199 (Alert), patient contact | IF active safety alert present | Immediately notify clinical team of risk factors |
title: Decision-Support Logic

content:
## Key Performance Indicators

The following indicators support quality improvement, safety monitoring, and outcome measurement across health and social care services implementing the Core Information Standard.

### Clinical Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.001 | Medication reconciliation completion rate | Patients with completed medication reconciliation (CISV2.DE1315) within 24h of admission | All admitted patients (CISV2.DE529) | By specialty, age group, admission type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.002 | Allergy documentation completeness | Patients with documented allergy status (CISV2.DE1265) | All active patients (CISV2.DE15) | By care setting, patient age | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.003 | Care plan review timeliness | Care plans reviewed by planned date (CISV2.DE1481) | All active care plans (CISV2.DE1432) | By care setting, complexity level | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.004 | Risk assessment currency | Patients with risk assessment (CISV2.DE1239) updated within 12 months | All patients with identified risks | By risk type, care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.005 | Safeguarding concern response time | Safeguarding cases (CISV2.DE379) with documented action within 24h | All new safeguarding concerns | By age group, concern type | Monthly |

### Safety & Governance Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.006 | Consent documentation compliance | Patients with documented consent (CISV2.DE215) for information sharing | All patients receiving multi-agency care | By care pathway, organisation | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.007 | Mental capacity assessment completion | Patients with mental capacity assessment (CISV2.DE262) where indicated | All patients with capacity concerns | By age group, condition type | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.008 | Critical alert acknowledgment rate | Clinical alerts (CISV2.DE199) acknowledged by clinicians | All generated critical alerts | By alert type, care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.009 | Discharge information completeness | Discharges (CISV2.DE558) with complete handover documentation | All hospital discharges | By specialty, destination | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.010 | Professional contact currency | Professional contacts (CISV2.DE453) updated within 6 months | All active professional relationships | By service type, patient complexity | Quarterly |

### Patient-Centered Care Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.011 | Person-authored content completion | Patients with completed "About me" section (CISV2.DE52) | All eligible patients | By age group, care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.012 | Goal achievement rate | Care goals (CISV2.DE1440) marked as achieved | All documented care goals | By goal type, time period | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.013 | Reasonable adjustment documentation | Patients with documented adjustments (CISV2.DE72) where required | All patients with identified adjustment needs | By adjustment type, organisation | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.014 | End-of-life preference documentation | Patients with documented care preferences (CISV2.DE1689) | All patients on end-of-life pathway | By care setting, age group | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.015 | Carer involvement documentation | Patients with documented carer details (CISV2.DE466) | All patients with identified carers | By care setting, dependency level | Quarterly |

### System Integration Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.016 | Data completeness rate | Records with ≥80% core data elements populated | All patient records (CISV2.DE15) | By organisation, record age | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.017 | Inter-service referral completeness | Referrals (CISV2.DE476) with complete clinical information | All inter-service referrals | By referral type, urgency | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.018 | Document accessibility rate | Clinical documents (CISV2.DE1788) accessible to authorized users | All shared clinical documents | By document type, sharing organisation | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND.019 | System interoperability success | Successful data exchanges between systems | All attempted data exchanges | By system pair, data type | Daily |
| CISV2.IND.020 | Data quality score | Records meeting data quality standards | All patient records | By data element group, organisation | Monthly |
title: Indicators & Performance Metrics

content:
## System Requirements

Implementation of the Core Information Standard requires adherence to specific functional and non-functional requirements to ensure safe, secure, and effective healthcare delivery.

### Functional Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR.001 | Patient Identity Management | System SHALL maintain unique patient identification using NHS Number (CISV2.DE15) with verification capabilities | CISV2.A | High |
| CISV2.FR.002 | Comprehensive Demographics Capture | System SHALL capture and maintain complete demographic information including names, addresses, and contact details | CISV2.A | High |
| CISV2.FR.003 | Clinical Information Recording | System SHALL support structured recording of problems, medications, allergies, and procedures with coded terminology | CISV2.A, CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.004 | Care Planning Functionality | System SHALL enable creation, maintenance, and review of person-centered care plans with goal tracking | CISV2.B | High |
| CISV2.FR.005 | Medication Management | System SHALL support complete medication lifecycle including prescribing, dispensing, administration, and reconciliation | CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.006 | Clinical Decision Support | System SHALL provide automated alerts and decision support based on patient data and clinical rules | CISV2.A, CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.007 | Professional Network Management | System SHALL maintain current professional contacts and care team information with role-based access | CISV2.B, CISV2.D | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.008 | Consent and Legal Status Tracking | System SHALL capture and enforce consent preferences and legal status including mental capacity assessments | CISV2.D | High |
| CISV2.FR.009 | Risk Assessment and Management | System SHALL support comprehensive risk assessment with automatic escalation for high-risk situations | CISV2.A, CISV2.B | High |
| CISV2.FR.010 | Document and Media Management | System SHALL enable secure storage, retrieval, and sharing of clinical documents and multimedia content | CISV2.D | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.011 | Assessment Tool Integration | System SHALL support structured assessment tools with automated scoring and trend analysis | CISV2.A, CISV2.B | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.012 | Safeguarding Alert System | System SHALL automatically generate safeguarding alerts based on patient demographics and risk factors | CISV2.A | High |
| CISV2.FR.013 | Care Transition Management | System SHALL facilitate secure information sharing during care transitions with appropriate consent controls | CISV2.D | High |
| CISV2.FR.014 | Appointment and Contact Tracking | System SHALL maintain comprehensive records of healthcare contacts and future appointments | CISV2.A, CISV2.B | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.015 | End-of-Life Care Planning | System SHALL support advance care planning including resuscitation preferences and care priorities | CISV2.B | Medium |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR.001 | Data Security and Encryption | System SHALL encrypt all patient data at rest and in transit using NHS-approved cryptographic standards | Security | High |
| CISV2.NFR.002 | Access Control and Authentication | System SHALL implement role-based access control with multi-factor authentication for healthcare professionals | Security | High |
| CISV2.NFR.003 | Audit Trail Completeness | System SHALL maintain comprehensive audit logs of all data access and modifications with user attribution | Security | High |
| CISV2.NFR.004 | GDPR Compliance | System SHALL comply with GDPR requirements including data subject rights and privacy by design principles | Regulatory | High |
| CISV2.NFR.005 | Clinical Safety Standards | System SHALL meet DCB 0129 and DCB 0160 clinical safety standards with appropriate risk management | Safety | High |
| CISV2.NFR.006 | System Availability | System SHALL maintain 99.9% uptime during core hours (08:00-18:00) with planned maintenance windows | Performance | High |
| CISV2.NFR.007 | Response Time Performance | System SHALL respond to user interactions within 2 seconds for 95% of standard queries | Performance | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.008 | Data Backup and Recovery | System SHALL implement automated daily backups with 4-hour recovery time objective for critical functions | Reliability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.009 | Interoperability Standards | System SHALL comply with HL7 FHIR R4 and NHS Digital interoperability standards | Integration | High |
| CISV2.NFR.010 | Scalability Requirements | System SHALL support concurrent access by up to 10,000 healthcare professionals without performance degradation | Performance | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.011 | User Interface Accessibility | System SHALL meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards for users with disabilities | Usability | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.012 | Mobile Device Support | System SHALL provide responsive design supporting tablets and smartphones for clinical use | Usability | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.013 | Data Retention Compliance | System SHALL enforce NHS data retention policies with automated archiving and disposal schedules | Regulatory | High |
| CISV2.NFR.014 | Business Continuity | System SHALL maintain essential functions during partial system failures with graceful degradation | Reliability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.015 | Integration Testing | System SHALL undergo comprehensive integration testing with existing NHS Digital services before deployment | Quality | High |
title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
